title: pachyrhizus angulatus
intro: Pachyrhizus angulatus, also known as the yam bean or Mexican turnip, is a perennial plant native to Central and South America. Its edible tubers and seeds possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Pachyrhizus angulatus include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and provide relief from pain and inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant's tubers and seeds are often dried and ground into powder or made into teas, capsules, or tinctures for consumption.
